('of the head').[[2]](#cite_note-2) The Latin phrase *[Roma Caput Mundi](/wiki/Caput_Mundi "Caput Mundi")* (lit. 'Rome head of the world') was already used by the poet [Ovid](/wiki/Ovid "Ovid") in the 1st century BC.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It originates out of a [classical European](/wiki/Classical_antiquity "Classical antiquity") understanding of the [known world](/wiki/Ecumene "Ecumene"): [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe"), [North Africa](/wiki/North_Africa "North Africa"), and [Southwest Asia](/wiki/Southwest_Asia "Southwest Asia"). The phrase is related to the enduring power of the city first as the capital of the [Republic](/wiki/Roman_Republic "Roman Republic") and the [Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"), and later as the centre of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), the capital of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), and the [most populous metropolitan area](/wiki/List_of_largest_cities "List of largest cities") in the world

A **capital city**, or just **capital**, is the [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") holding primary status in a [country](/wiki/Country "Country"), [state](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)"), [province](/wiki/Province "Province"), [department](/wiki/Department_(administrative_division) "Department (administrative division)"), or other subnational division, usually as its [seat of government](/wiki/Seat_of_government "Seat of government"). A capital is typically a [city](/wiki/City "City") that physically encompasses the government's offices and meeting places; the status as capital is often designated by law or a [constitution](/wiki/Constitution "Constitution"). In some [jurisdictions](/wiki/Jurisdiction "Jurisdiction"), including several countries, different branches of government are in different settlements, sometimes meaning there are [multiple official capitals](/wiki/List_of_countries_with_multiple_capitals "List of countries with multiple capitals"). In some cases, a distinction is made between the official (constitutional) capital and the seat of government.

English-language media often use the name of the capital [metonymically](/wiki/Metonymy "Metonymy") to refer to the government sitting there. For example, "London–Washington relations" is understood to mean diplomatic relations between the United Kingdom and the United States.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Tavares.Forum.Romanum.redux.jpg/250px-Tavares.Forum.Romanum.redux.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tavares.Forum.Romanum.redux.jpg)

[Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), as the capital of the [Roman Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"), acquired the nickname of *[Caput Mundi](/wiki/Caput_Mundi "Caput Mundi")* ("Capital of the world").

## Terminology and etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Terminology and etymology")]

The word *capital* derives from the Latin word *caput* (genitive *capitis*), meaning 'head', later borrowed from [Medieval Latin](/wiki/Medieval_Latin "Medieval Latin") **capitālis** ('of the head').[[2]](#cite_note-2) The Latin phrase *[Roma Caput Mundi](/wiki/Caput_Mundi "Caput Mundi")* (lit. 'Rome head of the world') was already used by the poet [Ovid](/wiki/Ovid "Ovid") in the 1st century BC.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It originates out of a [classical European](/wiki/Classical_antiquity "Classical antiquity") understanding of the [known world](/wiki/Ecumene "Ecumene"): [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe"), [North Africa](/wiki/North_Africa "North Africa"), and [Southwest Asia](/wiki/Southwest_Asia "Southwest Asia"). The phrase is related to the enduring power of the city first as the capital of the [Republic](/wiki/Roman_Republic "Roman Republic") and the [Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"), and later as the centre of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In several English-speaking [states](/wiki/Constituent_state "Constituent state"), the terms [county town](/wiki/County_town "County town") and [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") are also used in lower [administrative divisions](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division"). In some [unitary states](/wiki/Unitary_state "Unitary state"), subnational capitals may be known as 'administrative centres'. The capital is often the largest city of its constituent, though [not always](/wiki/List_of_countries_whose_capital_is_not_their_largest_city "List of countries whose capital is not their largest city").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/The_Forbidden_City_-_View_from_Coal_Hill.jpg/250px-The_Forbidden_City_-_View_from_Coal_Hill.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Forbidden_City_-_View_from_Coal_Hill.jpg)

[Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), as the last of the [Four Great Ancient Capitals](/wiki/Historical_capitals_of_China "Historical capitals of China") of [China](/wiki/China "China"), has served as the country's political centre for most of the past eight centuries.

## Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Origins")]

Historically, the major economic centre of a state or region has often become the focal point of political power, and became a capital through [conquest](/wiki/Conquest_(military) "Conquest (military)") or [federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Historical examples are [ancient Babylon](/wiki/Babylon "Babylon"), [ancient Athens](/wiki/Ancient_Athens "Ancient Athens"), [ancient Rome](/wiki/Ancient_Rome "Ancient Rome"), [Abbasid Baghdad](/wiki/Baghdad#History "Baghdad"), [Constantinople](/wiki/Constantinople "Constantinople"), [Chang'an](/wiki/Chang%27an "Chang'an"), and [ancient Cusco](/wiki/Cusco "Cusco"). The modern capital city has not always existed: in medieval Western Europe, an [itinerant (wandering) government](/wiki/Itinerant_court "Itinerant court") was common.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Tehran_Star_Trail.jpg/250px-Tehran_Star_Trail.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tehran_Star_Trail.jpg)

[Tehran](/wiki/Tehran "Tehran"), the capital of [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran") since the [Qajar Empire](/wiki/Qajar_Iran "Qajar Iran"), and the most populous city in [West Asia](/wiki/West_Asia "West Asia").[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The capital city attracts politically motivated people and those whose skills are needed for efficient [administration](/wiki/Administration_(government) "Administration (government)") of national or imperial governments, such as [lawyers](/wiki/Lawyer "Lawyer"), [political scientists](/wiki/Political_scientist "Political scientist"), [bankers](/wiki/Banking "Banking"), [journalists](/wiki/Journalist "Journalist"), and [public policy makers](/wiki/Public_policy_(law) "Public policy (law)"). Some of these cities are or were also [religious centres](/wiki/Holy_city "Holy city"),[[11]](#cite_note-11) e.g. [Constantinople](/wiki/Constantinople "Constantinople") (more than one religion), [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome")/[Vatican City](/wiki/Vatican_City "Vatican City") (the [Roman Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Church "Roman Catholic Church")), [Jerusalem](/wiki/Jerusalem "Jerusalem") (more than one religion), Babylon, Moscow (the [Russian Orthodox Church](/wiki/Russian_Orthodox_Church "Russian Orthodox Church")), Belgrade (the [Serbian Orthodox Church](/wiki/Serbian_Orthodox_Church "Serbian Orthodox Church")), Paris, and Beijing. In some countries, the capital has been changed for [geopolitical](/wiki/Geopolitics "Geopolitics") reasons; [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland")'s first city, [Turku](/wiki/Turku "Turku"), which had been the country's most important city since the Middle Ages and became capital in 1809, lost its position during the [Grand Duchy of Finland](/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Finland "Grand Duchy of Finland") in 1812, when [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki") was made the current capital of Finland by the Russian Empire.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

The convergence of political and economic or cultural power is by no means universal. Traditional capitals may be economically eclipsed by provincial rivals as is the case with [Nanjing](/wiki/Nanjing "Nanjing") by [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai"), [Quebec City](/wiki/Quebec_City "Quebec City") by [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"), and several [US state capitals](/wiki/US_state_capitals "US state capitals"). The [decline](/wiki/Societal_collapse "Societal collapse") of a dynasty or culture could also mean the extinction of its capital city, as occurred at Babylon[[14]](#cite_note-14) and [Cahokia](/wiki/Cahokia "Cahokia"). "Political nomadism" was practiced in [ancient Near East](/wiki/Ancient_Near_East "Ancient Near East") to increase ties between the ruler and the subjects.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Although many capitals are defined by constitution or legislation, many long-time capitals have no such legal designation, including [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern"), [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"), [Lisbon](/wiki/Lisbon "Lisbon"), [London](/wiki/London "London"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), and [Wellington](/wiki/Wellington "Wellington").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] They are recognized as capitals as a matter of convention, and because all or almost all the country's central political institutions, such as government departments, supreme court, legislature, embassies, etc., are located in or near them.

The earliest capital existing is [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus") which has existed since c.2500BC.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Modern capitals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Modern capitals")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Palace_of_Westminster_from_the_dome_on_Methodist_Central_Hall.jpg/250px-Palace_of_Westminster_from_the_dome_on_Methodist_Central_Hall.jpg)](/wiki/File:Palace_of_Westminster_from_the_dome_on_Methodist_Central_Hall.jpg)

[London](/wiki/London "London"), the capital of [England](/wiki/England "England") and the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), and the largest [metropolitan area](/wiki/London_metropolitan_area "London metropolitan area") in [Western Europe](/wiki/Western_Europe "Western Europe")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/La_Tour_Eiffel_vue_de_la_Tour_Saint-Jacques%2C_Paris_ao%C3%BBt_2014_%282%29.jpg/250px-La_Tour_Eiffel_vue_de_la_Tour_Saint-Jacques%2C_Paris_ao%C3%BBt_2014_%282%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:La_Tour_Eiffel_vue_de_la_Tour_Saint-Jacques,_Paris_ao%C3%BBt_2014_(2).jpg)

[Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), the capital of [France](/wiki/France "France"), and the largest metropolitan area in the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/eb/National_Bank_of_Egypt-2020.jpg/250px-National_Bank_of_Egypt-2020.jpg)](/wiki/File:National_Bank_of_Egypt-2020.jpg)

[Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo"), the capital of [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt"), and the largest metropolitan area in [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/Saint_Basil%27s_Cathedral_and_the_Red_Square.jpg/250px-Saint_Basil%27s_Cathedral_and_the_Red_Square.jpg)](/wiki/File:Saint_Basil%27s_Cathedral_and_the_Red_Square.jpg)

[Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"), the capital of [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), and the former capital of the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union")

Many modern capital cities are located near the centre of the country, so that they are more accessible to its population and have better protection from possible invasions. (See also [§ Capitals in military strategy](#Capitals_in_military_strategy)) The location may also be based on a compromise between two or more cities or other political divisions, historical reasons, or enough land was needed to deliberately build a new [planned city](/wiki/Planned_city "Planned city") for the capital.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The majority of national capitals are also the largest city in their respective countries. Modern examples are [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin"), [Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo"), [London](/wiki/London "London"), [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid"), [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"), [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), [Jakarta](/wiki/Jakarta "Jakarta"), [Metro Manila](/wiki/Metro_Manila "Metro Manila"), [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul"), and [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

[Counties](/wiki/Counties_of_the_United_Kingdom "Counties of the United Kingdom") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") have historic county towns, which are often not the largest settlement within the county and often are no longer administrative centres, as many historical counties are now only ceremonial, and administrative boundaries are different. The number of new capitals in the world increased substantially since the [Renaissance](/wiki/Renaissance "Renaissance") period, especially with the founding of independent nation-states since the eighteenth century.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

In [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), there is a [federal capital](/wiki/Ottawa "Ottawa"), while the ten [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Canada "Provinces of Canada") and three [territories](/wiki/Territories_of_Canada#Territories "Territories of Canada") each have capital cities. The states of such countries as [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") (including the famous cities of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") and [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo"), capitals of their respective states), and [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") also each have capital cities. For example, the six state capitals of Australia are [Adelaide](/wiki/Adelaide "Adelaide"), [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane"), [Hobart](/wiki/Hobart "Hobart"), [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne"), [Perth](/wiki/Perth "Perth"), and [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"). In Australia, the term "capital cities" is regularly used to refer to those six state capitals plus the federal capital [Canberra](/wiki/Canberra "Canberra"), and [Darwin](/wiki/Darwin,_Northern_Territory "Darwin, Northern Territory"), the capital of the [Northern Territory](/wiki/Northern_Territory "Northern Territory"). [Abu Dhabi](/wiki/Abu_Dhabi "Abu Dhabi") is the capital city of the [Emirate of Abu Dhabi](/wiki/Emirate_of_Abu_Dhabi "Emirate of Abu Dhabi") and also of the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") overall.

In unitary states which consist of multiple constituent nations, such as the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") and the [Kingdom of Denmark](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Denmark "Kingdom of Denmark"), each will usually have its own capital city. Unlike in [federations](/wiki/Federation "Federation"), there is usually not a separate national capital, but rather the capital city of one constituent nation will also be the capital of the state overall, such as [London](/wiki/London "London"), which is the capital of [England](/wiki/England "England") and of the United Kingdom. Similarly, each of the [autonomous communities of Spain](/wiki/Autonomous_communities_of_Spain "Autonomous communities of Spain") and [regions of Italy](/wiki/Regions_of_Italy "Regions of Italy") has a capital city, such as [Seville](/wiki/Seville "Seville") and [Naples](/wiki/Naples "Naples"), while [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid") is the capital of the [Community of Madrid](/wiki/Community_of_Madrid "Community of Madrid") and of the [Kingdom of Spain](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Spain "Kingdom of Spain") as a whole and [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome") is the capital of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") and of the region of [Lazio](/wiki/Lazio "Lazio").

In the [Federal Republic of Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), each of its constituent [states](/wiki/Federated_state "Federated state") (or [*Länder*](/wiki/States_of_Germany "States of Germany"), plural of *Land*) has its own capital city, such as [Dresden](/wiki/Dresden "Dresden"), [Wiesbaden](/wiki/Wiesbaden "Wiesbaden"), [Mainz](/wiki/Mainz "Mainz"), [Düsseldorf](/wiki/D%C3%BCsseldorf "Düsseldorf"), [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart"), and [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), as do all of the republics of the [Russian Federation](/wiki/Russian_Federation "Russian Federation"). The national capitals of Germany and Russia (the [Stadtstaat](/wiki/City-state#Non-sovereign_city-states "City-state") of [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin") and the [federal city](/wiki/Federal_cities_of_Russia "Federal cities of Russia") of [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow")) are also constituent states of both countries in their own right. Each of the [states of Austria](/wiki/States_of_Austria "States of Austria") and [cantons of Switzerland](/wiki/Cantons_of_Switzerland "Cantons of Switzerland") also have their own capital cities. [Vienna](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna"), the national capital of [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), is also one of the states, while [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern") is the (*de facto*) capital of both [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland") and of the [Canton of Bern](/wiki/Canton_of_Bern "Canton of Bern").

These cities satisfy one or both of the following criteria:

1. A deliberately [planned city](/wiki/Planned_city "Planned city") that was built expressly to house the [seat of government](/wiki/Seat_of_government "Seat of government"), superseding a capital city that was in an established [population center](/wiki/Center_of_population "Center of population"). There have been various reasons for this, including overcrowding in that major metropolitan area, and the desire to place the capital city in a location with a better climate (usually a less tropical one).
2. A town that was chosen as a compromise among two or more cities (or other political divisions), none of which was willing to concede to the other(s) the privilege of being the capital city. Usually, the new capital is geographically located roughly equidistant between the competing population centres.

### Compromise locations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Compromise locations")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/Parliamenthouse2.jpg/250px-Parliamenthouse2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Parliamenthouse2.jpg)

The [Australian Parliament](/wiki/Australian_Parliament "Australian Parliament") opened in the small town of [Canberra](/wiki/Canberra "Canberra") in 1927 as a compromise between the largest cities, [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") and [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne").

Some examples of the second situation (compromise locations) are:

* [Canberra](/wiki/Canberra "Canberra"), Australia, chosen as a compromise location between Melbourne and Sydney.
* [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C."), United States, founded as a compromise between more urbanized [Northern states](/wiki/Northern_United_States "Northern United States") and agrarian [Southern](/wiki/Southern_United_States "Southern United States") [slave states](/wiki/Slave_states_and_free_states "Slave states and free states") to share national power. The [Compromise of 1790](/wiki/Compromise_of_1790 "Compromise of 1790") resulted in the passage of the [Residence Act](/wiki/Residence_Act "Residence Act"), which approved the creation of a national capital on the [Potomac River](/wiki/Potomac_River "Potomac River") on land ceded from [Maryland](/wiki/Maryland "Maryland") and [Virginia](/wiki/Virginia "Virginia").[[20]](#cite_note-Crew124-20)
* [Frankfort, Kentucky](/wiki/Frankfort,_Kentucky "Frankfort, Kentucky"), midway between [Louisville](/wiki/Louisville,_Kentucky "Louisville, Kentucky") and [Lexington](/wiki/Lexington,_Kentucky "Lexington, Kentucky").
* [Ottawa](/wiki/Ottawa "Ottawa"), Ontario, Canada, along the boundary between the two former colonies that formed the core of pre-[Confederation](/wiki/Canadian_Confederation "Canadian Confederation") Canada: primarily English-speaking [Upper Canada](/wiki/Upper_Canada "Upper Canada") and primarily French-speaking [Lower Canada](/wiki/Lower_Canada "Lower Canada"). Today, this border separates the two most populous of Canada's ten modern provinces, [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario") and [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec").
* [Tallahassee, Florida](/wiki/Tallahassee,_Florida "Tallahassee, Florida"), chosen as the midpoint between [Pensacola](/wiki/Pensacola,_Florida "Pensacola, Florida") and [St. Augustine](/wiki/St._Augustine,_Florida "St. Augustine, Florida"), Florida's two largest cities at the time.
* [Wellington](/wiki/Wellington "Wellington") became the [capital city of New Zealand](/wiki/Capital_of_New_Zealand "Capital of New Zealand") in 1865. It lies at the southern tip of the [North Island](/wiki/North_Island "North Island") of New Zealand, the smaller of New Zealand's two main islands (which subsequently became the more populous island),[[21]](#cite_note-21) immediately across [Cook Strait](/wiki/Cook_Strait "Cook Strait") from the [South Island](/wiki/South_Island "South Island"). The previous capital, [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland"), lies much further north in the North Island; the move followed a long argument for a more central location for parliament.[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* [Managua](/wiki/Managua "Managua"), Nicaragua, chosen to appease rivals in [León](/wiki/Le%C3%B3n_(Nicaragua) "León (Nicaragua)") and [Granada](/wiki/Granada_(Nicaragua) "Granada (Nicaragua)"), which also were associated with the liberal and conservative political factions respectively.
* [Jefferson City, Missouri](/wiki/Jefferson_City,_Missouri "Jefferson City, Missouri"), was selected as the state capital in 1821, the year after Missouri was admitted to the Union, due to its central location within the state. It is almost halfway between Missouri's two largest cities, [Kansas City](/wiki/Kansas_City,_Missouri "Kansas City, Missouri") in the west and [St. Louis](/wiki/St._Louis "St. Louis") in the east, although Kansas City was not incorporated until 1850.

Changes in a nation's political regime sometimes result in the designation of a new capital. [Akmola](/wiki/Astana "Astana") (renamed Astana in 1998) became the capital of [Kazakhstan](/wiki/Kazakhstan "Kazakhstan") in 1997, following the [collapse of the Soviet Union](/wiki/Dissolution_of_the_Soviet_Union "Dissolution of the Soviet Union") in 1991. [Naypyidaw](/wiki/Naypyidaw "Naypyidaw") was founded in [Burma](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar")'s interior as the former capital, [Rangoon](/wiki/Rangoon "Rangoon"), was claimed to be overcrowded.[[23]](#cite_note-pedrosa-23)

## Unusual capital city arrangements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Unusual capital city arrangements")]

See also: [List of countries with multiple capitals](/wiki/List_of_countries_with_multiple_capitals "List of countries with multiple capitals")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d0/Bundesgericht-VD.jpg/250px-Bundesgericht-VD.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bundesgericht-VD.jpg)

The [Supreme Court](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_Switzerland "Supreme Court of Switzerland"), the seat of [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland")'s judiciary, is in [Lausanne](/wiki/Lausanne "Lausanne"), although the executive and legislature are located in [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/89/Parliament_House_Singapore.jpg/250px-Parliament_House_Singapore.jpg)](/wiki/File:Parliament_House_Singapore.jpg)

[Parliament House](/wiki/Parliament_House,_Singapore "Parliament House, Singapore") in [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"); as a [city-state](/wiki/City-state "City-state"), Singapore requires no specific capital.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/%D0%A6%D0%B5%D1%82%D0%B8%D1%9A%D0%B5%2C_%D0%A6%D1%80%D0%BD%D0%B0_%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0.jpg/250px-%D0%A6%D0%B5%D1%82%D0%B8%D1%9A%D0%B5%2C_%D0%A6%D1%80%D0%BD%D0%B0_%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0.jpg)](/wiki/File:%D0%A6%D0%B5%D1%82%D0%B8%D1%9A%D0%B5,_%D0%A6%D1%80%D0%BD%D0%B0_%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0.jpg)

The [Blue Palace](/wiki/Blue_Palace "Blue Palace"), the official residence of [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro")'s president, is in [Cetinje](/wiki/Cetinje "Cetinje"), although the executive and legislature are located in [Podgorica](/wiki/Podgorica "Podgorica").

A few nation-states have multiple capitals, and there are also several states that have no capital. Some have a city as the capital but with most government agencies elsewhere.

There is also a [ghost town](/wiki/Ghost_town "Ghost town") which is currently the *[de jure](/wiki/De_jure "De jure")* capital of a territory: [Plymouth](/wiki/Plymouth,_Montserrat "Plymouth, Montserrat") in [Montserrat](/wiki/Montserrat "Montserrat").

* [Belize](/wiki/Belize "Belize"): [Belmopan](/wiki/Belmopan "Belmopan") was designated the national capital of the then [British Honduras](/wiki/British_Honduras "British Honduras") in 1971, but most government offices and embassies are still located in [Belize City](/wiki/Belize_City "Belize City").
* [Canary Islands](/wiki/Canary_Islands "Canary Islands") ([Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain")): Until 1927, the capital of the [Province of Canarias](/wiki/Province_of_Canary_Islands "Province of Canary Islands") was [Santa Cruz de Tenerife](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_Tenerife "Santa Cruz de Tenerife"). When the Canary Islands became an autonomous community in 1982, Santa Cruz de Tenerife and [Las Palmas de Gran Canaria](/wiki/Las_Palmas_de_Gran_Canaria "Las Palmas de Gran Canaria") were both given capital status.[[24]](#cite_note-repetida_3-24)[[25]](#cite_note-repetida_1-25) There is currently a balance of institutions between the two capitals; the Canary Islands is the only autonomous community in Spain which has two capitals.
* [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"): [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago") is the capital even though the [National Congress of Chile](/wiki/National_Congress_of_Chile "National Congress of Chile") meets in [Valparaíso](/wiki/Valpara%C3%ADso "Valparaíso").
* [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"): [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague") is the capital city, however the [Constitutional Court](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_the_Czech_Republic "Constitutional Court of the Czech Republic"), [Supreme Court](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_the_Czech_Republic "Supreme Court of the Czech Republic") and [Supreme Administrative Court](/wiki/Supreme_Administrative_Court_of_the_Czech_Republic "Supreme Administrative Court of the Czech Republic") are all located in [Brno](/wiki/Brno "Brno").
* [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"): the [Supreme Court](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_Estonia "Supreme Court of Estonia") and the [Ministry of Education and Research](/wiki/Ministry_of_Education_and_Research_(Estonia) "Ministry of Education and Research (Estonia)") are located in [Tartu](/wiki/Tartu "Tartu").
* [France](/wiki/France "France"): The French constitution does not recognize any capital city in France. By law[[26]](#cite_note-26) [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") is the seat of both houses of Parliament (the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(France) "National Assembly (France)") and the [Senate](/wiki/Senate_(France) "Senate (France)")), but their joint congresses are held at the [Palace of Versailles](/wiki/Palace_of_Versailles "Palace of Versailles"). In case of emergency, the seat of the constitutional powers can be transferred to another town, in order for the Houses of Parliament to sit in the same location as the [President](/wiki/President_of_France "President of France") and [Cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_of_France "Cabinet of France").
* [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"): The official capital [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin") is home to the parliament and the highest bodies of the executive branch (consisting of the ceremonial [presidency](/wiki/President_of_Germany "President of Germany") and effective [chancellery](/wiki/Chancellor_of_Germany "Chancellor of Germany")). Various ministries are located in the former [West German](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") capital of [Bonn](/wiki/Bonn "Bonn"), which now has the title "[Federal City](/wiki/Federal_City "Federal City")". The [Federal Constitutional Court](/wiki/Federal_Constitutional_Court_of_Germany "Federal Constitutional Court of Germany") has its seat in [Karlsruhe](/wiki/Karlsruhe "Karlsruhe") which, as a consequence, is sometimes called Germany's "judicial capital"; none of Germany's highest judicial organs are located in Berlin. Various German government agencies are located in other parts of Germany.
* [India](/wiki/India "India"):
  + [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh"): [Hyderabad](/wiki/Hyderabad "Hyderabad") was the *de jure* capital of the state until 2024, while [Amaravati](/wiki/Amaravati "Amaravati") is the *de facto* seat of government since 2014. The Governor of Andhra Pradesh has his official residence in [Vijayawada](/wiki/Vijayawada "Vijayawada")
  + [Chhattisgarh](/wiki/Chhattisgarh "Chhattisgarh"): [Raipur](/wiki/Raipur "Raipur") is the administrative and legislative capital, while the high court (judiciary capital) is located in [Bilaspur](/wiki/Bilaspur,_Chhattisgarh "Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh"). The proposed future capital is [Nava Raipur](/wiki/Naya_Raipur "Naya Raipur").
  + [Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(union_territory) "Jammu and Kashmir (union territory)"): [Srinagar](/wiki/Srinagar "Srinagar") serves as the summer capital of the state while [Jammu](/wiki/Jammu "Jammu") is the winter capital. Every six months, the entire state machinery [shifts](/wiki/Darbar_Move "Darbar Move") from one city to another.
  + [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"): [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram") is the administrative and legislative capital of the state, while the high court is located in [Ernakulam](/wiki/Ernakulam "Ernakulam").
  + [Himachal Pradesh](/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh"): [Shimla](/wiki/Shimla "Shimla") is the primary capital city. [Dharamshala](/wiki/Dharamshala "Dharamshala"), which is also the headquarters of the [Central Tibetan Administration](/wiki/Central_Tibetan_Administration "Central Tibetan Administration"), is the second winter capital of the state.
  + [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh"): [Bhopal](/wiki/Bhopal "Bhopal") is the administrative and legislative capital of the state, while the high court is located in [Jabalpur](/wiki/Jabalpur "Jabalpur").
  + [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab,_India "Punjab, India") and [Haryana](/wiki/Haryana "Haryana"): Both states share [Chandigarh](/wiki/Chandigarh "Chandigarh") as their capital city. The city itself is administered as a [Union territory](/wiki/Union_territory "Union territory").
  + [Odisha](/wiki/Odisha "Odisha"): [Bhubaneswar](/wiki/Bhubaneswar "Bhubaneswar") is the administrative and legislative capital of the state, while the high court is located in [Cuttack](/wiki/Cuttack "Cuttack").
  + [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"): [Jaipur](/wiki/Jaipur "Jaipur") is the administrative and legislative capital of the state, while the high court is located in [Jodhpur](/wiki/Jodhpur "Jodhpur").
  + [Uttarakhand](/wiki/Uttarakhand "Uttarakhand"): [Dehradun](/wiki/Dehradun "Dehradun") is the administrative and legislative capital, while the high court is located in [Nainital](/wiki/Nainital "Nainital"). The proposed future capital is [Gairsain](/wiki/Gairsain "Gairsain").
  + [Ladakh](/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh"): [Leh](/wiki/Leh "Leh") and [Kargil](/wiki/Kargil_district "Kargil district") serve as joint capitals of the Union Territory.
* [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea"): [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul") remains as the capital and seat of the government's branches, but many government agencies have moved to [Sejong City](/wiki/Sejong_City "Sejong City").
* [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"): [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur") is the [constitutional](/wiki/Constitution_of_Malaysia "Constitution of Malaysia") capital, home of the [King](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Agong "Yang di-Pertuan Agong"), and seat of [Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Malaysia "Parliament of Malaysia"), but the federal administrative centre and judiciary have been moved 30 kilometres (19 mi) south to [Putrajaya](/wiki/Putrajaya "Putrajaya").
* [Monaco](/wiki/Monaco "Monaco"), [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"), and the [Vatican City](/wiki/Vatican_City "Vatican City") are [city-states](/wiki/City-state "City-state"), and thus do not contain any distinct capital city as a whole. However, in Singapore's case, the main judiciary and legislative offices are located in the [Downtown Core](/wiki/Downtown_Core "Downtown Core"). Similarly, while [Victoria](/wiki/Victoria,_Hong_Kong "Victoria, Hong Kong") was the capital of colonial Hong Kong, the heart of old Victoria, now known as [Central](/wiki/Central,_Hong_Kong "Central, Hong Kong"), serves as the seat of government offices today. Vatican City, however, is the [religious centre](/wiki/Holy_city "Holy city") of the [Roman Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Church "Roman Catholic Church") and houses the offices and departments of [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") which serves as the government of both the city-state and worldwide Catholic Church.
* [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro"): The official capital [Podgorica](/wiki/Podgorica "Podgorica") is home to the parliament and the executive, but the seat of the [presidency](/wiki/President_of_Montenegro "President of Montenegro") is in the former royal capital of [Cetinje](/wiki/Cetinje "Cetinje").
* [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") ([Burma](/wiki/Burma "Burma")): [Naypyidaw](/wiki/Naypyidaw "Naypyidaw") was designated the national capital in 2005, the same year it was founded, but most government offices and embassies are still located in [Yangon](/wiki/Yangon "Yangon") ([Rangoon](/wiki/Rangoon "Rangoon")).
* [Nauru](/wiki/Nauru "Nauru"): Nauru, a [microstate](/wiki/Microstate "Microstate") of only 21 square kilometres (8.1 sq mi), has no distinct capital city, but has a capital district instead.
* [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"):
  + National capital: Presidential Decree No. 940, issued on 24 June 1976, designates the whole of [National Capital Region](/wiki/Metro_Manila "Metro Manila") (NCR) or Metro Manila as the seat of government, with the [City of Manila](/wiki/Manila "Manila") as the country's capital.[[27]](#cite_note-27) Some national government institutions and agencies are located within the Manila capital city, while others are scattered on other parts of the metropolitan area. The presidential palace ([Malacañang Palace](/wiki/Malaca%C3%B1ang_Palace "Malacañang Palace"), serving as the seat of the [President of the Philippines](/wiki/President_of_the_Philippines "President of the Philippines")) and the [Supreme Court](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_the_Philippines "Supreme Court of the Philippines") are located within the capital city while the two houses of Congress are located outside the capital Manila but within the metropolis of the same name.
  + [Cavite](/wiki/Cavite "Cavite"): [Imus](/wiki/Imus "Imus") is designated as the provincial capital, while government offices are in [Trece Martires](/wiki/Trece_Martires "Trece Martires").[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"):
  + National capital: the [Portuguese constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_Portugal "Constitution of Portugal") has no reference to a capital. Although [Lisbon](/wiki/Lisbon "Lisbon") is home to the [Parliament](/wiki/Assembly_of_the_Republic_(Portugal) "Assembly of the Republic (Portugal)"), the [President](/wiki/President_of_Portugal "President of Portugal")'s and the [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Portugal "Prime Minister of Portugal")'s official residences, all the [Government](/wiki/Portuguese_Government "Portuguese Government")'s departments, all the [embassies](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_in_Portugal "List of diplomatic missions in Portugal") and the highest courts, no Portuguese official document states that Lisbon is the national capital.[[29]](#cite_note-29)
  + [Azores](/wiki/Azores "Azores"): since the establishment of local autonomy in 1976, the Azores has three designated regional capital cities: [Ponta Delgada](/wiki/Ponta_Delgada "Ponta Delgada") at [São Miguel Island](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Miguel_Island "São Miguel Island") (seat of the [Autonomous Government](/wiki/Presidents_of_the_Government_of_the_Azores "Presidents of the Government of the Azores")); [Horta](/wiki/Horta,_Azores "Horta, Azores") at [Faial Island](/wiki/Faial_Island "Faial Island") (seat of the [Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_the_Azores "Legislative Assembly of the Azores")); and [Angra do Heroísmo](/wiki/Angra_do_Hero%C3%ADsmo "Angra do Heroísmo") at [Terceira Island](/wiki/Terceira_Island "Terceira Island") (seat of the judiciary and the historical capital of the Azores, in addition to being the seat of the [Roman Catholic Diocese of Angra](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Angra "Roman Catholic Diocese of Angra")).
* [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka"): [Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte](/wiki/Sri_Jayawardenepura_Kotte "Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte") is designated the administrative capital and the location of the parliament, while the former capital, [Colombo](/wiki/Colombo "Colombo"), is now designated as the "commercial capital".[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31) However, many government offices are still located in Colombo. Both cities are in the [Colombo District](/wiki/Colombo_District "Colombo District").
* [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa"): The administrative capital is [Pretoria](/wiki/Pretoria "Pretoria"), the legislative capital is [Cape Town](/wiki/Cape_Town "Cape Town"), and the judicial capital is [Bloemfontein](/wiki/Bloemfontein "Bloemfontein"). This is the outcome of the compromise that created the [Union of South Africa](/wiki/Union_of_South_Africa "Union of South Africa") in 1910. Despite Bloemfontein's status as the judicial capital, the country's highest court, the [Constitutional Court of South Africa](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_South_Africa "Constitutional Court of South Africa"), sits in its largest city, [Johannesburg](/wiki/Johannesburg "Johannesburg").
* [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland"): [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern") is the *[Federal City](/wiki/Federal_City "Federal City")* of Switzerland and functions as *de facto* capital. However, the [Swiss Supreme Court](/wiki/Federal_Supreme_Court_of_Switzerland "Federal Supreme Court of Switzerland") is located in [Lausanne](/wiki/Lausanne "Lausanne") which is also the [Olympic Capital](/wiki/International_Olympic_Committee "International Olympic Committee").
  + [Canton of Zürich](/wiki/Canton_of_Z%C3%BCrich "Canton of Zürich"): [Zürich](/wiki/Z%C3%BCrich "Zürich") is the *de facto* capital of the canton, but the cantonal constitution makes no mention of a capital city.[[32]](#cite_note-32)
* [Tanzania](/wiki/Tanzania "Tanzania"): [Dodoma](/wiki/Dodoma "Dodoma") was designated the national capital in 1996, but some of the government offices and almost all embassies are still located in [Dar es Salaam](/wiki/Dar_es_Salaam "Dar es Salaam").[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"):
  + [California](/wiki/California "California"): The state executive and legislative branches and most government agencies are based in [Sacramento](/wiki/Sacramento "Sacramento") but the [California Supreme Court](/wiki/California_Supreme_Court "California Supreme Court") is headquartered in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco") with secondary meeting places in Sacramento and [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
  + [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"): [Springfield](/wiki/Springfield,_Illinois "Springfield, Illinois") has the seats of the branches of state government and serves as the official state capital. However various Illinois government officials primarily reside in or are primarily active in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago").[[35]](#cite_note-INN-35)[[36]](#cite_note-Gauen-36) (see: [Government of Illinois § Capital](/wiki/Government_of_Illinois#Capital "Government of Illinois") for a further explanation)
  + [Louisiana](/wiki/Louisiana "Louisiana"): The state executive and legislative branches and most government agencies are based in [Baton Rouge](/wiki/Baton_Rouge,_Louisiana "Baton Rouge, Louisiana"), but the [Louisiana Supreme Court](/wiki/Louisiana_Supreme_Court "Louisiana Supreme Court") is located in [New Orleans](/wiki/New_Orleans "New Orleans").
  + [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"): The state capital and government are headquartered in [Albany](/wiki/Albany,_New_York "Albany, New York"), but many officials are mostly active in or live in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
  + [Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pennsylvania "Pennsylvania"): The state capital is [Harrisburg](/wiki/Harrisburg,_Pennsylvania "Harrisburg, Pennsylvania") but each one of the state Supreme Court and its two appellate courts holds hearings in the three cities of Harrisburg, [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia"), and [Pittsburgh](/wiki/Pittsburgh "Pittsburgh"). Also, most statewide elected officials and officers who are based in Southeast Pennsylvania (City of Philadelphia, [Bucks County](/wiki/Bucks_County,_Pennsylvania "Bucks County, Pennsylvania"), [Montgomery County](/wiki/Montgomery_County,_Pennsylvania "Montgomery County, Pennsylvania"), [Delaware County](/wiki/Delaware_County,_Pennsylvania "Delaware County, Pennsylvania"), and [Chester County](/wiki/Chester_County,_Pennsylvania "Chester County, Pennsylvania")) prefer working mostly in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Capitals that are not the seat of government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Capitals that are not the seat of government")]

There are several countries where, for various reasons, the official capital and [seat of government](/wiki/Seat_of_government "Seat of government") are separated:

* [Benin](/wiki/Benin "Benin"): [Porto-Novo](/wiki/Porto-Novo "Porto-Novo") is the official capital, but [Cotonou](/wiki/Cotonou "Cotonou") is the seat of government.
* [Bolivia](/wiki/Bolivia "Bolivia"): [Sucre](/wiki/Sucre "Sucre") is the [constitutional](/wiki/Constitution_of_Bolivia "Constitution of Bolivia") capital, and the [supreme tribunal of justice](/wiki/Supreme_Tribunal_of_Justice_(Bolivia) "Supreme Tribunal of Justice (Bolivia)") is located in Sucre, making it the judicial capital. The *[Palacio Quemado](/wiki/Palacio_Quemado "Palacio Quemado")*, the [national congress](/wiki/Plurinational_Legislative_Assembly "Plurinational Legislative Assembly") and [national electoral court](/wiki/Plurinational_Electoral_Organ "Plurinational Electoral Organ") are located in [La Paz](/wiki/La_Paz "La Paz"), making it the seat of government.
* [Ivory Coast](/wiki/Ivory_Coast "Ivory Coast"): [Yamoussoukro](/wiki/Yamoussoukro "Yamoussoukro") was designated the national capital in 1983, but most government offices and embassies are still located in [Abidjan](/wiki/Abidjan "Abidjan").
* [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"): [Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam") is the constitutional national capital even though the [Dutch government](/wiki/Cabinet_of_the_Netherlands "Cabinet of the Netherlands"), the [parliament](/wiki/States_General_of_the_Netherlands "States General of the Netherlands"), the [supreme court](/wiki/Hoge_Raad_der_Nederlanden "Hoge Raad der Nederlanden"), the [Council of State](/wiki/Council_of_State_of_the_Netherlands "Council of State of the Netherlands"), and the [work palace](/wiki/Noordeinde_Palace "Noordeinde Palace") of the [King](/wiki/Willem-Alexander_of_the_Netherlands "Willem-Alexander of the Netherlands") are all located in [The Hague](/wiki/The_Hague "The Hague"), as are all the [embassies](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_in_the_Netherlands "List of diplomatic missions in the Netherlands"). (*For more details see: [Capital of the Netherlands](/wiki/Capital_of_the_Netherlands "Capital of the Netherlands")*.)

Some historical examples of similar arrangements, where the recognized capital was not the official seat of government:

* [Kingdom of England](/wiki/Kingdom_of_England "Kingdom of England"): The traditional capital was the [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London"), while [Westminster](/wiki/Westminster "Westminster"), outside of the boundaries of the City of London, was the seat of government. They are both today part of the urban core of [Greater London](/wiki/Greater_London "Greater London").
* [Kingdom of France](/wiki/Kingdom_of_France "Kingdom of France"): The traditional capital was [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), though from 1682 to 1789 the seat of government was at the [Palace of Versailles](/wiki/Palace_of_Versailles "Palace of Versailles"), located in a rural area southwest of Paris.

### Disputed capitals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Disputed capitals")]

* [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus "Cyprus") and [Northern Cyprus](/wiki/Northern_Cyprus "Northern Cyprus"): [Nicosia](/wiki/Nicosia "Nicosia"), "the last divided capital",[[37]](#cite_note-37) is divided in two by the [United Nations Buffer Zone in Cyprus](/wiki/United_Nations_Buffer_Zone_in_Cyprus "United Nations Buffer Zone in Cyprus") (Green Line). Both the [Republic of Cyprus](/wiki/Republic_of_Cyprus "Republic of Cyprus"),[[38]](#cite_note-38) which has *de facto* control of the south, and the largely unrecognized [Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus](/wiki/Turkish_Republic_of_Northern_Cyprus "Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus"),[[39]](#cite_note-39) which has *de facto* control of [North Nicosia](/wiki/North_Nicosia "North Nicosia"), claim the entire city as their capital.
* [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") and [Palestine](/wiki/Palestine "Palestine"): Both the [Government of Israel](/wiki/Government_of_Israel "Government of Israel")[[40]](#cite_note-40) and the [Palestinian Authority](/wiki/Palestinian_Authority "Palestinian Authority")[[41]](#cite_note-41) claim [Jerusalem](/wiki/Jerusalem "Jerusalem") as their capital. Jerusalem serves as Israel's capital, with the presidential residence, government offices, supreme court and parliament ([Knesset](/wiki/Knesset "Knesset")) located there, while the Palestinian Authority has no *de facto* or *de jure* control over any of Jerusalem. Many countries, with the notable exception of the United States, which recognizes Jerusalem as the capital of Israel,[[42]](#cite_note-42) take [the position that the final status of Jerusalem](/wiki/Status_of_Jerusalem "Status of Jerusalem") is unsettled pending future [negotiations](/wiki/Israeli-Palestinian_negotiations "Israeli-Palestinian negotiations"). Most countries maintain their [diplomatic missions to Israel](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_in_Israel "List of diplomatic missions in Israel") in [Tel Aviv](/wiki/Tel_Aviv "Tel Aviv"), while [diplomatic missions to Palestine](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_in_Palestine "List of diplomatic missions in Palestine") are in various places such as [Ramallah](/wiki/Ramallah "Ramallah"), [Gaza City](/wiki/Gaza_City "Gaza City"), [Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo") and [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus").

## Capital as symbol

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Capital as symbol")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6c/View_of_Mariehamn%2C_2022.jpg/250px-View_of_Mariehamn%2C_2022.jpg)](/wiki/File:View_of_Mariehamn,_2022.jpg)

[Mariehamn](/wiki/Mariehamn "Mariehamn"), capital city of [Åland](/wiki/%C3%85land "Åland"), a [demilitarized](/wiki/Demilitarized "Demilitarized") archipelago with [self-governance](/wiki/Self-governance "Self-governance")

With the rise of the modern [nation-state](/wiki/Nation_state "Nation state"), the capital city has become a [symbol](/wiki/Symbol "Symbol") for the [state](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state") and its [government](/wiki/Government "Government"), and imbued with political meaning. Unlike [medieval](/wiki/Medieval "Medieval") capitals, which were declared wherever a [monarch](/wiki/Monarch "Monarch") held their court, the selection, relocation, founding, or capture of a modern capital city is a highly symbolic event. For example:

* The ruined and almost uninhabited [Athens](/wiki/Athens "Athens") was made capital of [newly independent Greece](/wiki/Independence_of_Greece "Independence of Greece") in 1834, four years after the country gained its independence, with the [romantic](/wiki/Romanticism "Romanticism") notion of reviving the glory of [Ancient Greece](/wiki/Ancient_Greece "Ancient Greece").[[43]](#cite_note-43) Similarly, following the [Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War") and [German reunification](/wiki/German_reunification "German reunification"), [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin") once again became the capital of Germany.[[44]](#cite_note-44) Other restored capital cities include [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow") after the [October Revolution](/wiki/October_Revolution "October Revolution").
* A symbolic relocation of a capital city to a geographically or demographically peripheral location may be for either [economic](/wiki/Economic_geography "Economic geography") or [strategic](/wiki/Military_geography "Military geography") reasons (sometimes known as a *forward capital* or *spearhead capital*). [Peter the Great](/wiki/Peter_the_Great "Peter the Great") moved his government from [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow") to [Saint Petersburg](/wiki/Saint_Petersburg "Saint Petersburg") to give the [Russian Empire](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire") a [European](/wiki/Europe "Europe") orientation.[[45]](#cite_note-45) The economically significant city of [Nafplio](/wiki/Nafplion "Nafplion") became the first capital of [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), when Athens was an unimportant village.[[46]](#cite_note-46) The [Ming](/wiki/Ming_dynasty "Ming dynasty") emperors moved their capital to [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing") from the more central [Nanjing](/wiki/Nanjing "Nanjing") to help supervise the border with the Mongols. During the 1857 rebellion, [Indian rebels](/wiki/India "India") considered [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi") their capital, and [Bahadur Shah Zafar](/wiki/Bahadur_Shah_Zafar "Bahadur Shah Zafar") was proclaimed emperor, but the ruling [British](/wiki/British_India "British India") had their capital in [Calcutta](/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata"). In 1877, the British formally held a '[Durbar](/wiki/Delhi_Durbar "Delhi Durbar")' in Delhi, proclaiming [Queen Victoria](/wiki/Queen_Victoria "Queen Victoria") as '[Empress of India](/wiki/Empress_of_India "Empress of India")'. Delhi finally became the colonial capital after the [Coronation Durbar](/wiki/Delhi_Durbar "Delhi Durbar") of King-Emperor [George V](/wiki/George_V "George V") in 1911, continuing as independent India's capital from 1947. Other examples include [Abuja](/wiki/Abuja "Abuja"), [Astana](/wiki/Astana "Astana"), [Brasília](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia "Brasília"), [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki"), [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad"), [Naypyidaw](/wiki/Naypyidaw "Naypyidaw"), and [Yamoussoukro](/wiki/Yamoussoukro "Yamoussoukro").
* The selection or founding of a "neutral" capital city, one unencumbered by regional or political identities, was meant to represent the unity of a new state when [Ankara](/wiki/Ankara "Ankara"), [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern"), [Brasília](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia "Brasília"), [Canberra](/wiki/Canberra "Canberra"), [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid"), [Ottawa](/wiki/Ottawa "Ottawa") and [Washington](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.") became capital cities. Sometimes, the location of a new capital city was chosen to terminate actual or potential squabbling between various entities, such as in the cases of Brasília, Canberra, Ottawa, Washington, Wellington and [Managua](/wiki/Managua "Managua").
* The British-built town of [New Delhi](/wiki/New_Delhi "New Delhi") represented a simultaneous break and continuity with the past, the location of Delhi being where many imperial capitals were built (Indraprastha, Dhillika, and Shahjahanabad) but the actual capital being the new British-built town designed by [Edwin Lutyens](/wiki/Edwin_Lutyens "Edwin Lutyens").
* Wellington, on the southwestern tip of the [North Island](/wiki/North_Island "North Island") of New Zealand, replaced the much more northerly city of [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland") to place the national capital close to the [South Island](/wiki/South_Island "South Island") and hence to placate its residents, many of whom had sympathies with separatism.
* During the [American Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"), tremendous resources were expended to defend Washington, D.C., which bordered on the [Confederate States of America](/wiki/Confederate_States_of_America "Confederate States of America") (with the [Commonwealth of Virginia](/wiki/Virginia "Virginia")), from Confederate attack even though the relatively small federal government could easily have been moved elsewhere. Likewise, great resources were expended by the Confederacy in defending the Confederate capital from attack by the Union, in its exposed location of [Richmond, Virginia](/wiki/Richmond,_Virginia "Richmond, Virginia"), barely 100 miles (160 km) south of Washington, D.C.[[47]](#cite_note-47)
* Two national capitals refer to another sovereign state. The name of [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), the capital of [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"), is thought to be derived from *Taani linn*, originally meaning "Danish Castle" and now "Danish Town" in [Estonian](/wiki/Estonian_language "Estonian language"), named after the [Toompea Castle](/wiki/Toompea_Castle "Toompea Castle"), which [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") controlled in 1219–1227, 1238–1332 and in 1340–1346.[[48]](#cite_note-48) [Port of Spain](/wiki/Port_of_Spain "Port of Spain"), the capital of [Trinidad and Tobago](/wiki/Trinidad_and_Tobago "Trinidad and Tobago"), was named so in [Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language") by the first settlers from [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") in the 16th century.[[49]](#cite_note-49) See [List of national capital city name etymologies](/wiki/List_of_national_capital_city_name_etymologies "List of national capital city name etymologies") for more.

## Capitals in military strategy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Capital_city&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Capitals in military strategy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/df/Fall-of-constantinople-22.jpg/250px-Fall-of-constantinople-22.jpg)](/wiki/File:Fall-of-constantinople-22.jpg)

[Constantinople](/wiki/Constantinople "Constantinople"), the capital of the [Byzantine Empire](/wiki/Byzantine_Empire "Byzantine Empire"), was the final part of the empire to fall to the [Ottoman Turks](/wiki/Ottoman_Turks "Ottoman Turks") due to its strong defences.

The capital city is usually but not always a primary target in a war, as capturing it usually guarantees capture of much of the enemy government, victory for the attacking forces, or at the very least demoralization for the defeated forces.

In [ancient China](/wiki/Ancient_China "Ancient China"), where governments were massive centralized bureaucracies with little flexibility on the provincial level, a [dynasty](/wiki/Chinese_Dynasties "Chinese Dynasties") could easily be toppled with the fall of its capital. In the [Three Kingdoms](/wiki/Three_Kingdoms "Three Kingdoms") period, both [Shu](/wiki/Shu_Han "Shu Han") and [Wu](/wiki/Eastern_Wu "Eastern Wu") fell when their respective capitals of [Chengdu](/wiki/Chengdu "Chengdu") and [Jianye](/wiki/Jianye_District "Jianye District") fell. The [Ming dynasty](/wiki/Ming_dynasty "Ming dynasty") relocated its capital from [Nanjing](/wiki/Nanjing "Nanjing") to [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), where they could more effectively control the generals and troops guarding the borders from [Mongols](/wiki/Mongols "Mongols") and [Manchus](/wiki/Manchus "Manchus"). The Ming was destroyed when [Li Zicheng](/wiki/Li_Zicheng "Li Zicheng") took their seat of power, and this pattern repeats itself in Chinese history, until the fall of the traditional [Confucian](/wiki/Confucian "Confucian") monarchy in the 20th century. After the [Qing dynasty](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty")'s collapse, decentralization of authority and improved transportation and communication technologies allowed both the [Chinese Nationalists](/wiki/Chinese_Nationalists "Chinese Nationalists") and [Chinese Communists](/wiki/Chinese_Communists "Chinese Communists") to rapidly relocate capitals and keep their leadership structures intact during the great crisis of [Japanese invasion](/wiki/Second_Sino-Japanese_War "Second Sino-Japanese War").

National capitals were arguably less important as military objectives in other parts of the world, including the West, because of socioeconomic trends toward localized authority, a strategic modus operandi especially popular after the development of [feudalism](/wiki/Feudalism "Feudalism") and reaffirmed by the development of democratic and capitalistic philosophies. In 1204, after the Latin [Crusaders](/wiki/Fourth_Crusade "Fourth Crusade") captured the [Byzantine](/wiki/Byzantine_Empire "Byzantine Empire") capital, [Constantinople](/wiki/Constantinople "Constantinople"), Byzantine forces were able to regroup in several provinces; provincial noblemen managed to reconquer the capital after 60 years and preserve the empire for another 200 years after that. The [British](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire") forces sacked various [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") capitals repeatedly during the [Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War") and [War of 1812](/wiki/War_of_1812 "War of 1812"), but American forces could still carry on fighting from the countryside, where they enjoyed support from local governments and the traditionally independent civilian frontiersmen. Exceptions to these generalizations include highly centralized states such as [France](/wiki/France "France"), whose centralized bureaucracies could effectively coordinate far-flung resources, giving the state a powerful advantage over less coherent rivals, but risking utter ruin if the capital were taken.
